revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image for a game-development pixel-art asset.

Use the attached reference image as the strict character identity reference. Treat this as re-posing the same character into a sprite sheet, not designing a new character.

Profile: snes16_platformer_v1
Preset: player_idle_4
Asset kind: animated-sprite
Asset type: sprite
Exact canvas / sheet size: 128x32 pixels
Grid: 1 row x 4 columns
Frame size: 32x32 pixels
Frames: 4
Background: solid #FF00FF chroma key filling all empty space, no gradients.

Create a crisp 16-bit SNES-style pixel-art idle loop sprite sheet for a bright mythical-Japan action RPG character named Suzushiro. She is a small elderly shrine maiden matching the attached reference: round white hair bun, deeply kind wrinkled smile, white kosode top, vermilion hakama, slim gently stooped elderly build, holding a short wooden staff with small bells. Front view facing the viewer.

Very important proportions: about 3 heads tall with a SMALL head relative to the body. Do NOT draw a big head. Do NOT draw a chibi or super-deformed mascot. Keep a slim elderly build. Same identity, same small head size, same head-to-body ratio, same body width, same limb thickness, and same exact scale in every frame. The standing body height in pixels must match across all frames. Feet stay on the same ground line in every frame.

Sheet layout: one horizontal row of four equal 32x32 cells, with no visible grid lines or borders.
Frame 1: neutral idle stance, front-facing, staff upright.
Frame 2: subtle slow breath pose, shoulders and sleeves shift slightly, bells tilt gently.
Frame 3: eyes closed in a warm kind smile, subtle staff/bell tilt, same scale and ground line.
Frame 4: return toward neutral, loops cleanly with frame 1.

Pixel-art constraints: crisp low-resolution pixels, no antialiasing blur, consistent palette, fine readable pixel clusters, dark one-pixel outline where useful, top-left light source, bright cheerful colors. Keep clear solid #FF00FF chroma-key margin between the figure and every cell edge. Every frame must stay inside its own 32x32 cell.

Sheet cleanliness rules: Do not draw cell separator lines, grid lines, frame borders, frame numbers, labels, text, UI, signatures, or watermarks anywhere. No detached fragments: no isolated pixel islands, seam slivers, dust specks, leftover effect residue, or random loose pixels. Solid magenta must fill all empty space.

Avoid all protected or derivative likenesses: no Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos, or likeness. No samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no bird or companion creature, no letter emblems.
